title: Python backend integration for macOS device owner authentication
goal: Wire the macOS native authentication helper into the Python secret backend and CLI so plaintext secret reveal requires OS-level device owner authentication.
inputs: Existing secret CLI flows in src/envrcctl/cli.py
Phase 2 native helper contract and invocation interface
outputs: Python helper invocation path for authenticated Keychain reads
Backend API for authenticated secret reveal
CLI integration for macOS-only authenticated reveal
Deprecation-compatible handling for legacy plaintext flags
Tests covering backend invocation and failure mapping
acceptance_criteria: `envrcctl secret reveal VAR` retrieves plaintext only after successful macOS device owner authentication
Plaintext reveal fails closed when authentication is unavailable, cancelled, or the helper is missing
Existing unauthenticated TTY-based plaintext output is removed or routed through the authenticated path on macOS
Errors do not include secret values
Unsupported platforms return a clear error for authenticated reveal
Automated tests cover success and failure paths for Python-side integration
dependencies: Native macOS authentication helper implemented and runnable
constraints: Do not rely on TTY/interactivity checks as the security boundary
Do not leak secrets via stdout/stderr except on successful explicit reveal
Preserve existing non-reveal backend behavior unless explicitly changed by the plan
implementation_notes: Add an explicit authenticated retrieval path rather than overloading generic get behavior invisibly
Validate helper path and invocation arguments before execution
Capture subprocess output safely and map native helper exit conditions to concise user-facing errors
Prefer `secret reveal` as the primary plaintext path; legacy `--plain`/`--show`/`--force-plain` should become authenticated aliases or be deprecated behind the same auth flow
Keep platform gating explicit and fail closed on non-macOS systems

task_breakdown.title: Define Python-side authenticated reveal interface
outputs: Backend method or helper wrapper for authenticated retrieval
Clear separation between operational get and plaintext reveal get

task_breakdown.title: Implement helper invocation in macOS backend
outputs: Subprocess invocation of native helper with service/account arguments

task_breakdown.title: Add CLI command for authenticated reveal
outputs: User-facing errors for unsupported platform or auth failure

task_breakdown.title: Migrate legacy plaintext flags onto authenticated flow
outputs: Deprecated but safe handling for `--plain`, `--show`, and `--force-plain`

task_breakdown.title: Add Python-side tests
outputs: CLI tests for reveal success/failure behavior
Coverage for helper-missing, auth-cancelled, and unsupported-platform cases

automated: Run unit tests for backend and CLI reveal flows
Verify mocked helper success returns only the secret value
Verify mocked helper failures return sanitized error messages
manual: On macOS, confirm `secret reveal` triggers device owner authentication
Confirm cancellation does not print plaintext
Confirm legacy plaintext flags no longer bypass authentication

risks.risk: Native helper output or failures may be surfaced unsafely
risks.mitigation: Centralize subprocess result handling and sanitize all error paths
Route legacy flags through the same authenticated flow and emit deprecation guidance

risks.risk: Non-macOS code paths regress while adding macOS-specific logic
risks.mitigation: Keep platform-specific behavior isolated in backend implementation and add explicit tests

definition_of_done: Python backend can invoke the macOS native helper for authenticated reveal
CLI exposes a safe authenticated plaintext reveal command
Legacy plaintext paths no longer bypass OS authentication on macOS
Tests pass for the new integration layer
Ticket status can move from checking to verified after acceptance criteria review
